domain: Browser-based educational block coding puzzle game with SQLite-backed telemetry and analytics
constraints: Exactly three playable puzzles with lockable progression teaching sequencing, loops, and conditionals
Workspace must use an `On Start` root, left command library (movement, actions, control, logic, sensing) and right active code area
Run controls must expose Play, Reset, and speed toggle with highly visible styling
Failure feedback must show `Oops!` plus contextual hint for target not reached, wrong item/order, obstacle collision, and unhandled condition
Game scenes must follow provided layout references and asset placement rules using local assets in `public/assets/...`
Visual identity must be polished cartoon style with pink/violet palette and cohesive UI matching gameplay
Gameplay execution must compile connected blocks, animate tile-based movement with collisions, pickups, treatments, facing direction, and guard against infinite loops
success_criteria: Three puzzles, each completable, unlock sequentially, and enforce concept focus (sequencing, loops, conditionals)
Play action animates scene and evaluates success; incorrect runs show `Oops!` with hint
SQLite database auto-initializes on first run and persists attempts, events, and movements for completed runs
Telemetry captures sessions, puzzle lifecycle, block edits, run start/end, movement steps, collisions, pickups, treatments, hints, completion, and code snapshot
Analytics dashboard surfaces aggregates, puzzle detail, attempt history, event stream, and movement replay sourced from DB
expected_outputs: Next.js 16 TypeScript frontend implementing landing page, level select, puzzle workspace, execution engine, failure/success UI, and analytics views
Backend API routes (`/api/session/start`,`/api/session/end`,`/api/events/batch`,`/api/analytics/*`) using SQLite via better-sqlite3
Puzzle data definitions (id, story, goal, scene, grid, entities, available blocks, constraints, success checks, hints) enabling data-driven expansion
SQLite schema and migration/initialization logic covering users, sessions, puzzles, attempts, events, movements, puzzle_progress
Telemetry ingestion logic and analytics readers wired between frontend and backend
stack_requirements: Next.js 16.x with unified frontend/backend TypeScript codebase
WebGL-capable 2D rendering for animated scenes and sprite handling
Drag-and-drop block editor (custom or Blockly) with substantial custom styling compatible with Next.js 16
validation_expectations: Build and runtime checks for the Next.js app to confirm frontend/backend integration
Automated or manual verification that SQLite database creates itself on startup
Tests or scripts confirming telemetry insertion for runs, including attempts/events/movements rows
Verification that analytics queries can read stored data and replay movements from DB records
End-to-end flow validation: start session, play puzzle, log events, view analytics, ensure speed/reset controls and warnings behave as specified
repo_constraints: Use canonical asset directories (`public/assets/backgrounds`, `public/assets/sprites`, `design/layout_refs`) and replicate provided layout reference when composing scenes
Application source, puzzle data, telemetry logic, and analytics must reside within this repository; planner must specify concrete relative file paths
Backend functionality must leverage Next.js server routes; no external services or non-SQLite storage
Adhere to accessibility requirements (keyboard navigation, color contrast, no color-only cues, optional TTS for goals)
No proprietary third-party characters/branding; assets must remain original or repository-provided
missing_information: Specific narrative text, dialog, and hint wording for each puzzle beyond high-level goals
Exact analytics metrics/visual designs beyond required aggregates, details, and replays
Precise UI layout measurements beyond provided layout reference for puzzle 1; extensions to later puzzles must be inferred
assumptions: Provided assets and layout reference are sufficient to build all three puzzle scenes by extrapolation
Custom WebGL or canvas rendering can coexist within Next.js pages without additional build constraints
Telemetry volume is manageable within SQLite and better-sqlite3 without extra queueing beyond optional debouncing for high-frequency UI events
